title: What Is a Multimeter? A Complete Guide to How It Works
description: Learn what a multimeter is, how it works, and what it measures — voltage, current, and resistance — in this beginner-to-advanced guide for engineers and electricians.
excerpt: A multimeter is the most essential instrument in any electrician's or engineer's toolkit. This guide breaks down exactly how it works — from basic physics to practical usage.

title: How to Use a Clamp Meter: Step-by-Step Guide for Electricians
description: A step-by-step guide on how to use a clamp meter safely to measure AC and DC current without breaking a circuit.
excerpt: Clamp meters let you measure current safely without cutting a wire. Here's everything you need to know — from jaw placement to reading AC vs DC current.

title: Ohm's Law Explained: The Physics Behind Every Electrical Tester
description: Understand Ohm's Law — V = IR — and how it underpins every measurement made by multimeters, clamp meters, and insulation testers used by engineers worldwide.
excerpt: Every reading on a multimeter or clamp meter traces back to one equation: V = IR. Master Ohm's Law and you'll understand exactly what every tester is measuring.

title: Continuity Testing: What It Is and Why Every Electrician Needs It
description: Learn what continuity testing is, how it works, and how to use a multimeter to find open circuits, blown fuses, and broken wiring.
excerpt: Continuity testing is the quickest way to confirm a complete electrical path. Learn the physics behind it and how to run the test correctly with any multimeter.

title: Clamp Meter vs Multimeter: Which Tester Do You Actually Need?
description: Compare clamp meters and multimeters side by side — find out which instrument to use for current measurement, voltage testing, resistance checks, and field electrical work.
excerpt: Both measure electrical quantities — but they work very differently. Here's a practical breakdown of when to reach for a clamp meter versus a multimeter.

title: How to Use an Oscilloscope: A Complete Beginner's Guide
description: Learn how to use an oscilloscope step by step — how to connect probes, set voltage and time scales, read waveforms, and measure frequency, period, and amplitude.
excerpt: An oscilloscope turns invisible electrical signals into visible waveforms you can measure. This complete guide takes you from unpacking to your first real measurement.

title: Insulation Resistance Testing: What It Is and How to Do It
description: A complete guide to insulation resistance testing — what it measures, how a megohmmeter works, correct test voltages for cables and motors, and how to interpret results.
excerpt: Insulation resistance testing is the single most important test you can run on any electrical installation. Learn the test voltages, procedures, and how to read the results.

title: DC Power Supply Explained: Types, Uses, and How to Choose One
description: Everything you need to know about DC power supplies — linear vs switching, regulated vs unregulated, constant current vs constant voltage, and how to select one for your bench.
excerpt: A DC power supply is the foundation of any electronics lab or test bench. This guide breaks down every type, what the specs actually mean, and how to choose the right one.

title: Earth Resistance Testing: Why It Matters and How to Measure It
description: Learn what earth resistance is, why it must be measured, the 3-point fall-of-potential method, and how to use an earth tester to ensure electrical safety compliance.
excerpt: A poor earth connection is the most invisible and most dangerous electrical fault. This guide explains earth resistance, accepted limits, and how to measure it correctly.

title: Soldering Station vs Soldering Iron: Which Should You Buy?
description: Compare soldering stations and soldering irons on temperature control, tip types, safety, and value.
excerpt: Both heat up and melt solder — but the similarity ends there. Here's a clear breakdown of when a basic iron is enough and when you need a proper soldering station.

title: What Is a Lux Meter? How to Measure Light Intensity Correctly
description: Learn what a lux meter measures, how it works, correct measurement technique, standard lux requirements for offices, factories, and outdoor spaces.
excerpt: Lux is the unit of illuminance — and measuring it correctly requires more than just pointing a sensor at a light. Here's everything you need to know about lux meters.

title: Transformer Oil BDV Test: What It Is and How to Perform It
description: Learn what the transformer oil breakdown voltage (BDV) test is, why it is critical for transformer maintenance, the test procedure, and how to interpret results per IS standards.
excerpt: The BDV test is the primary health check for transformer oil — and a failing result is an early warning of catastrophic failure. Here's exactly how to run and read the test.

title: How to Read an Oscilloscope Waveform: Voltage, Time, Frequency
description: Learn how to read an oscilloscope screen — measure amplitude, period, frequency, duty cycle, and understand common waveform types with practical examples.
excerpt: The oscilloscope screen is a graph — once you know what the axes mean and what each waveform type looks like, every signal tells a story. Here's how to read it.

title: AC vs DC Electricity: What's the Difference and Which Is Dangerous?
description: Understand the fundamental difference between alternating current (AC) and direct current (DC) — how each is generated, measured, used, and why both matter for electrical safety.
excerpt: AC powers your home, DC powers your phone — but the difference goes much deeper than that. Here's the physics, the history, and why every electrician must understand both.

title: How to Test Motor Windings: Insulation, Resistance, and Continuity
description: Learn how to test electric motor windings using a multimeter and insulation tester — checking continuity, winding resistance balance, and insulation resistance to earth.
excerpt: A failed motor is expensive. A bad winding test costs minutes. This guide covers the three tests that tell you whether a motor is healthy, degraded, or ready to fail.

title: Voltage Drop: What It Is, Why It Matters, and How to Calculate It
description: Learn what voltage drop is, what causes it, how to calculate it using Ohm's Law, and the maximum permissible limits for LT installations per IS 732.
excerpt: Voltage drop is the silent cause of overheating motors, dim lighting, and equipment faults. Here's how to calculate it and keep it within safe limits.

title: True RMS vs Average RMS: Why It Matters for Your Multimeter
description: Learn the difference between True RMS and average-responding multimeters, when it matters, and why using the wrong type gives wildly inaccurate readings on modern loads.
excerpt: Most cheap multimeters assume a perfect sine wave. Modern loads — VFDs, inverters, switch-mode supplies — are not sine waves. Here's why True RMS matters.

title: How to Test a Relay: Continuity, Coil Resistance, and Operate Voltage
description: Learn how to test an electromechanical relay with a multimeter — checking coil resistance, normally open and normally closed contact continuity, and pull-in voltage.
excerpt: A faulty relay is one of the hardest faults to find without the right test procedure. Here's a step-by-step guide to testing any relay with basic instruments.

title: Power Factor Explained: What It Is, Why It Drops, and How to Correct It
description: Understand power factor — the ratio of real to apparent power — why it drops in industrial installations, the financial penalties, and how capacitor banks correct it.
excerpt: A low power factor is costing your facility money every hour. Here's what power factor actually means, how to measure it, and how to correct it.

title: Phase Sequence Testing: Why It Matters for 3-Phase Motors and How to Check It
description: Learn what phase sequence is, why reversing it damages motors and equipment, and how to use a phase sequence indicator to verify the correct RYB sequence.
excerpt: Connect three-phase power in the wrong sequence and the motor runs backwards — or worse. Here's why phase sequence matters and how to check it in 30 seconds.

title: How to Choose a Multimeter: Complete Buying Guide for 2026
description: A practical buying guide to choosing the right multimeter — covering CAT safety ratings, auto-ranging, True RMS, resolution, and what to look for at every price point.
excerpt: There are hundreds of multimeters on the market. This guide cuts through the noise — CAT ratings, True RMS, resolution, and what actually matters for your type of work.

title: What Is Electrical Earthing? Types, Methods, and Why It's Non-Negotiable
description: Learn what electrical earthing is, the different earthing systems (TN, TT, IT), why it is the most critical safety measure in any installation, and how it is tested.
excerpt: Earthing is not optional — it is the last line of defence between a fault and a fatal shock. Here's how every earthing system works and why testing it matters.

title: Desoldering Guide: Wick, Pump, and Station — How to Remove Solder Cleanly
description: Learn the three desoldering methods — solder wick, solder sucker, and desoldering station — with step-by-step technique for PCB components and through-hole parts.
excerpt: Removing solder cleanly is harder than applying it. Here's when to use wick, when to use a pump, and when you need a proper desoldering station.

title: Short Circuit vs Open Circuit vs Ground Fault: What's the Difference?
description: Understand the three fundamental electrical faults — short circuit, open circuit, and ground fault — how each occurs, what damage it causes, and how to identify it.
excerpt: Three faults. Three completely different failure modes. Three different ways to find them. Every electrician needs to know the difference — and how to test for each.

title: Digital vs Analogue Multimeter: Which Is Better for Your Work?
description: Compare digital (DMM) and analogue (AVM) multimeters — accuracy, response time, reading live signals, resistance measurement, and which type suits which application.
excerpt: Analogue multimeters were the standard for decades. Digital took over — but analogue still has real advantages in specific situations. Here's an honest comparison.

title: LCR Meter Guide: How to Measure Inductance, Capacitance, and Resistance
description: A complete guide to LCR meters — what they measure, why test frequency matters, how to measure capacitors and inductors correctly, 4-wire Kelvin connection, and open/short compensation.
excerpt: An LCR meter reveals what a multimeter cannot — the true impedance parameters of any passive component at the frequency it actually operates at. Here's how to use one correctly.

title: How to Test a Capacitor: Multimeter, ESR Meter, and LCR Meter Methods
description: Learn how to test a capacitor correctly — visual inspection, multimeter capacitance mode, resistance leakage check, ESR testing for electrolytics, and LCR meter analysis.
excerpt: A capacitor that reads the right value on a multimeter can still be completely failed — if its ESR is too high. Here's every method for testing capacitors properly.

title: What Is a Megger? How to Use a Megohmmeter for Insulation Testing
description: Learn what a megger (megohmmeter) is, why you need high voltage to test insulation, which test voltages to use, how to run the test correctly, and how to interpret the Polarisation Index.
excerpt: A megger tests the one thing a regular multimeter can't — whether your cable or motor insulation will survive operating voltage without breaking down.

title: Function Generator Guide: What It Does and How to Use One
description: A complete guide to function generators — waveform types, key specifications, how to set frequency and amplitude correctly, frequency sweep, DDS vs analogue, and practical bench tests.
excerpt: A function generator is the signal source that makes every other bench instrument more useful. Here's what it produces, what the specs mean, and how to use it correctly.

title: How to Test a Transformer: Winding Resistance, Insulation, and Turns Ratio
description: A complete guide to transformer testing — winding resistance (open circuit and short circuit detection), insulation resistance, turns ratio test, polarity test, and no-load test.
excerpt: Transformers fail in five distinct ways and each requires a different test to detect. Here's the complete electrical test sequence for any transformer.

title: How to Test a Battery: Load Test, Internal Resistance, and State of Charge
description: Learn how to test batteries correctly — why open-circuit voltage misleads, how to load test, how to measure internal resistance, and how to assess lead-acid and lithium-ion batteries.
excerpt: A battery that reads 12.5 V can be completely useless. Voltage alone doesn't tell you if a battery can deliver current. Here's how to actually test one.

title: Harmonic Distortion Explained: What THD Is and Why It Damages Your Electrical System
description: Understand harmonic distortion — what harmonics are, why VFDs and switch-mode supplies generate them, how they damage capacitors, transformers and motors, how to measure THD, and how to mitigate it.
excerpt: Harmonics are invisible, inaudible, and expensive. They overheat cables, destroy capacitor banks, and trip equipment. Here's what they are and what to do about them.

title: How to Measure Frequency: Multimeter, Oscilloscope, and Frequency Counter Methods
description: Learn how to measure electrical frequency correctly — using a multimeter's Hz mode, an oscilloscope, a frequency counter, or a clamp meter — with guidance on VFD verification and generator testing.
excerpt: Frequency measurement is simple on a pure sine wave and tricky on VFD outputs. Here's which instrument to use for each application and the common mistakes to avoid.

title: How to Choose a Clamp Meter: Complete Buying Guide for 2026
description: A practical buying guide to choosing a clamp meter — True RMS vs average-responding, jaw size, AC/DC capability, frequency response, CAT safety ratings, in-rush measurement, and use-case recommendations.
excerpt: There are clamp meters at every price point — but the one that reads 30% low on your VFD loads is not saving you money, it's costing you accuracy. Here's what actually matters.

title: Electrical Panel Testing: How to Inspect and Test a Distribution Board
description: A complete guide to testing electrical distribution boards — visual inspection, voltage testing, load current measurement, insulation resistance testing, RCD testing, and thermal imaging.
excerpt: The distribution board is the most common location for electrical faults, overheating, and arc flash. Here's the complete systematic test procedure from external check to insulation test.
